value_chain_position: EUV and DUV lithography equipment — the singular, non-bypassable chokepoint every leading-edge chip fab must use; no fab can manufacture <7nm chips without ASML's tools. Load-bearing position is a literal EUV monopoly.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING/STABLE on rising revenue (pricing power, not commoditization): FY23 ~51.3% -> FY24 ~51.3% -> FY25 52.8% (quarterly prints across the window 51.6%-54%); management guides 51-53% for 2026 with a CMD path to 56-60% by 2030 as High-NA/EUV mix enriches. Moat lie-detector reads POSITIVE.
bull_thesis: ASML is the most load-bearing company in the entire AI buildout — a literal EUV monopoly, the non-bypassable chokepoint through which every <7nm chip must pass, with a load_bearing_score of 5. Narrative hardened across five calls from 'we cannot confirm 2026 growth' (Q2'25) to 'memory sold out for 2026, supply constraint beyond 2026' (Q1'26), and financials corroborate: FY25 rev EUR32.67B (+15.6%), NI EUR9.61B (+27%), EPS EUR24.71 (+28.4%), TTM EPS EUR25.87. GM held/expanded at 52.8% on rising revenue = pricing power. Returns are software-like (ROE 54.1%, ROIC 30.6%, FCF margin 32.6%), balance sheet net CASH EUR5.67B, exemplary capital allocation (buybacks into strength, growing dividend, strategic 11% Mistral stake). Theme is web-verified durable and multi-year: backlog EUR38.8B into 2027, 2026 guide raised to EUR36-40B, EUV output >=60 units 2026 / >=80 2027, SK Hynix record ~$8B EUV order, ~$400B 2026 hyperscaler AI capex, NVIDIA wafer-intensity 4x by 2027 layered on rising EUV layer-count. High-NA (EXE) is a fresh S-curve with design wins across all five leading-edge buyers; advanced packaging (XT260) opens an adjacent TAM. Elite reinvestment economics with a 2030 CMD target of EUR44-60B at 56-60% GM. Conditions: AI capex stays multi-year; China/MATCH-Act stays a bounded single-digit drag; High-NA reaches HVM.
bear_thesis: Almost entirely a PRICE case — which is why it bites. At EUR1,863 ASML trades 63x FCF and ~35% ABOVE every intrinsic model (Buffett FV EUR1,121, DCF EUR542), +50% above its 200d SMA at 97% of its 52-wk range, and BELOW the EUR1,694 analyst target (-9%). The multiple discounts the 2030 aspiration, not the high-teens trajectory. Risks: (1) AI capex is a CAPEX cycle and litho equipment is the most violently cyclical link — one year of hyperscaler/memory digestion turns +15% growth flat and a 63x multiple compresses brutally (derate to a still-premium 30x TTM EPS ~ EUR776). 'Sold out 2026' visibility does NOT extend to 2027+. (2) China live/escalating: EUR1.4B de-booking already realized (Q2'25); China >25% of 2025 rev -> ~20% in 2026; proposed MATCH Act cuts DUV sales AND high-margin China service. (3) Litho-intensity 'pause' at 2nm until gate-all-around (~1.4nm) — management conceded 'a bit of a pause.' (4) High-NA is margin-DILUTIVE today, still phase I/II — the 2030 56-60% GM thesis is unbanked. (5) Customer concentration (handful of leading-edge buyers, TSMC largest) + 1,700-job reorg at a cyclical peak. Confirmation: any 2027 capex-digestion signal, hard MATCH-Act enactment, or a single EUV-bookings miss.
sop_bull: EUR1,250/sh (earnings-power-weighted). Anchor: TTM EPS EUR25.87 x 46-50x monopoly multiple = EUR1,190-1,294 + net cash EUR14.6/sh. Segment EV/Sales cross-check (FY25): NXE EUV Low-NA EUR10.45B x14=EUR146B; ArFi immersion EUR10.31B x8=EUR82B; EXE High-NA EUR1.16B x16=EUR19B; KrF EUR1.0B x6=EUR6B; Metro/inspect EUR0.83B x10=EUR8B; ArF Dry EUR0.43B x5=EUR2B; I-Line EUR0.31B x4=EUR1B; Installed Base/service EUR8.19B x11=EUR90B; sum ~EUR354B EV + net cash = ~EUR925/sh (sales-view understates 30%+ FCF margins).
sop_bear: EUR620/sh (theme pauses 12-18mo). Anchor: normalize TTM EPS -5% to ~EUR24.6 x derated 25-30x = EUR615-738 + net cash EUR14.6. Segment EV/Sales deep-derate (volume haircut + multiple compression, ArFi/DUV most China-exposed): NXE x0.80x8=EUR67B; ArFi x0.75x5=EUR39B; EXE x0.70x7=EUR6B; KrF x0.85x4=EUR3B; Metro x0.85x6=EUR4B; ArF Dry+I-Line ~EUR2B; Installed Base/service EUR8.19B x8=EUR66B; sum ~EUR187B EV + net cash = ~EUR494/sh tail. Even the bear is a profitable net-cash monopoly — downside is multiple compression on a great business, not impairment.
sop_breakdown: Reconciled base case blends the verified-durable theme (weights toward bull) against the valuation reality (price 35% above intrinsic). Earnings-power anchor: TTM EPS EUR25.87 x ~44x (premium-but-not-peak monopoly multiple) = ~EUR1,138, + net cash EUR14.6/sh = ~EUR1,150 — which independently corroborates the Buffett intrinsic FV of EUR1,121. By parts, the value is overwhelmingly the two leading-edge engines (NXE EUV Low-NA ~EUR10.45B rev + ArFi immersion ~EUR10.31B rev = 85% of system sales) plus the EUR8.19B high-margin Installed Base/service annuity; EXE High-NA (EUR1.16B today) is option value on the 2027-28 ramp. Net cash EUR5.67B (EUR14.6/sh) is a genuine add-back. Base-case fair value EUR1,150 vs spot EUR1,863 = the stock is ~38% over the by-parts fair value.
risk_reward: UNFAVORABLE at spot. Downside-to-base-fair EUR1,863 -> EUR1,150 = -38%; downside-to-bear EUR620 = -67% (tail). Upside-to-bull EUR1,250 from spot is NEGATIVE (-33%) — even the favorable SoP sits below the price. Risk/reward from EUR1,863 is roughly -38% (base) / -67% (bear) downside vs ~0 upside-to-fair = deeply negative skew. The thesis only becomes attractive on a ~35-40% drawdown (toward EUR1,150) — a China/MATCH-Act flush or a 2027-capex scare is the entry, not today's print.
peer_comps_note: theme_map true_competitors AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/TOELY trade ~20-28x fwd EPS as cyclical semicap; ASML's ~35x+ fwd (and the 44x I assign at base-fair) is a justified monopoly premium given the literal EUV monopoly, 52.8% GM vs peers' 45-50%, and 30%+ FCF margin. But spot 63x P/FCF / ~72x TTM EPS is ABOVE even the defensible monopoly premium — the multiple, not the moat, is the problem. Sanity check: no competitor (Nikon/Canon in DUV; nobody in EUV) can route around ASML, so the premium is real; it is the DEGREE of premium at EUR1,863 that fails.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way on quality — it is looking the wrong way on PRICE, and in the bulls' favor the wrong way is 'too expensive.' Sell-side consensus target is EUR1,694, BELOW the EUR1,863 spot (-9%) and analyst-target velocity is +22% (chasing the price up). The factually-stretched assumption embedded in the EUR1,863 print is that 2026's 'sold out' visibility extends seamlessly into a multi-year, non-cyclical 2027-2030 ramp at the CMD's EUR44-60B / 56-60% GM — i.e. the market is capitalizing the 2030 aspiration today at 63x FCF. Our verified facts support theme DURABILITY (backlog into 2027, $400B hyperscaler capex, SK Hynix $8B order) but NOT the absence of a capex-cycle pause, and management itself only confirms 2026, explicitly declining to underwrite 2027 ('we refrain from making further comments'). The expectations arbitrage is therefore inverted vs a classic disruptor: the crowd is RIGHT on the business and WRONG (too generous) on the multiple. There is no positive consensus_delta to harvest at spot.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) risks that hit BEFORE any re-rate, all to the downside of an already-stretched multiple: (1) MATCH Act / China DUV+service export restrictions — a dated legislative risk that would cut a high-margin revenue stream (China ~20% of 2026 sales); any enactment headline is a fast 5-10% drawdown. (2) Q2'26 / Q3'26 prints: ASML has STOPPED disclosing quarterly order numbers (Q1'26 was the first quarter without them), removing the bookings cushion the bulls leaned on and raising the penalty for any soft revenue/GM guide. (3) GM step-down risk: H2 GM guided lower on High-NA dilution (a recurring pattern every year). (4) Any TSMC/memory 2027-capex-digestion signal. There is NO cash-burn or maturity risk (net cash EUR5.67B, FCF margin 32.6%) — the valley of death here is purely a MULTIPLE event on a stock at 97% of its 52-wk range, not a solvency event.
positioning_washout: No dividend-cut or yield-base washout mechanic applies — ASML grows its dividend and the shareholder base is growth/quality, not yield. The relevant mechanical dynamic is the reverse: ASML is a mega-cap index and semiconductor-ETF/AI-basket anchor, so passive AND momentum flows have pushed it to +50% over its 200d SMA at 97% of range with analyst-target velocity +22%. That crowding cuts both ways — a China/MATCH-Act or capex-scare catalyst could trigger momentum and AI-basket DE-grossing that overshoots fundamentals to the downside, which is precisely the flush a patient buyer should wait for. No forced-liquidation in the base case; instead, watch for a momentum unwind as the entry.
forcing_function: Hard dated events: (1) Q2 2026 earnings (~mid-July 2026) — the next read on whether 2026 EUR36-40B holds and the first real test of the no-order-disclosure regime. (2) MATCH Act legislative progress through 2026 — a binding-vote or rule date is the single catalyst that could supply a 35-40% entry by repricing China. (3) Q4'26 / January 2027 call — the first hard read on 2027, which is where the 'capex-cycle vs secular' debate gets adjudicated; management has deferred all 2027 commentary, so this is the date the consensus is forced to confront the durability question. For a BUYER, the forcing function to BUY is a drawdown toward EUR1,150 driven by (2) or a 2027 capex scare.
moderator_conclusion: ASML is, on the merits, the highest-quality name a disruptor lens can surface: a load_bearing_score of 5, a literal EUV monopoly nobody can route around (if it disappeared, the entire sub-7nm world stops), an EXPANDING gross-margin trajectory (51.3% -> 52.8%) on rising revenue that proves pricing power rather than commoditization, 30.6% ROIC with a net-cash balance sheet, and a theme I web-verified as durable and multi-year (backlog EUR38.8B into 2027, 2026 guide raised to EUR36-40B, ~$400B 2026 hyperscaler AI capex, SK Hynix's record ~$8B EUV order, High-NA design wins across all five leading-edge buyers). The theme is NOT a capex air-pocket away from rollover in 2026 — memory is sold out and constrained beyond 2026. This is a STRENGTHENING franchise (Interrogator credibility 5). And yet the verdict is B, conviction 3, because a great business at the wrong price is a poor investment: at EUR1,863 the stock trades ~38% ABOVE my reconciled by-parts fair value of EUR1,150 (which independently matches the Buffett intrinsic of EUR1,121), 63x FCF, BELOW the EUR1,694 sell-side target, at 97% of its 52-week range — even the FAVORABLE SoP (EUR1,250) sits below spot, so the risk/reward is negative-skewed (-38% to base, -67% bear tail, ~0 upside-to-fair). catalyst_status is SOFT_EXTENDED (the 'catalyst' here is just the serially-extended AI-capex narrative, not a dated binding event) and per the rules it does NOT drive the verdict — but the theme-durability and moat verdict is unambiguously POSITIVE; it is purely valuation that caps this. WATCHLIST FOR CAPITULATION: the exact event to wait for is a MATCH-Act enactment or a 2027-capex-digestion scare that flushes the stock toward EUR1,150 — at which point this becomes an aggressive-entry, conviction-5 secular monopoly. value_conviction is 2 (the value case alone, ignoring theme/regime: forensic quality is pristine but the margin of safety is deeply NEGATIVE at -38%), and it diverges from conviction 3 precisely because the franchise quality and theme durability earn the name a watchlist slot the raw valuation would not.
